note: New files only. Do not touch bot_core.py, core/risk.py, or any existing file. The watcher runs as a subprocess and writes a shared state file. Bot integration is a separate task (RESOLUTION_WATCHER_INTEGRATE_001).

context.problem: The launch_all.py launcher already tries to start 'python -m integration.resolution_watcher' but the integration/ package does not exist. When a Polymarket market resolves (e.g. COL loses → YES token → price drops to 0), the orderbook has no valid bids, _held_bid() returns None, check_exit() returns False early (core/risk.py:178), and the position gets stuck open forever. This caused trades 200, 205, 213, and 216 to require manual intervention. The resolution_watcher is the missing piece that tells the bot a market has settled so it can force-close at the final resolution price.

existing_infrastructure.launcher: launch_all.py already has the entry: {name: 'resolution_watcher', command: ['python', '-m', 'integration.resolution_watcher'], log_file: 'logs/resolution_watcher.log'}. No changes needed to launch_all.py.
existing_infrastructure.gamma_api: https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets?id={market_id} — returns JSON with fields: resolved (bool), outcomePrices (list of strings e.g. ['1', '0'] or ['0', '1']), slug, closed, active. Already used by core/discovery.py.
existing_infrastructure.db: core/db.fetch_open_trades() returns all open Trade objects with market_id field. Import path: from core.db import fetch_open_trades
existing_infrastructure.shared_state_file: runtime/resolved_markets.json — does not exist yet, watcher creates it

implementation.integration/__init__.py: Empty file. Just makes integration/ a Python package.

integration/resolution_watcher.py.description: Standalone subprocess. Polls Gamma API every 60s for all market_ids that have open trades. Writes runtime/resolved_markets.json with resolution status. Runs forever until killed by launcher watchdog.

output_schema.description: Dict keyed by market_id. Only includes markets where resolved=true.

logic: 1. On each poll cycle: call fetch_open_trades() to get currently open market_ids.
2. Load existing runtime/resolved_markets.json (if exists) — skip re-fetching market_ids already in it with resolved=true.
3. For each new market_id not yet confirmed resolved: GET https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets?id={market_id}
4. Parse response: j[0].get('resolved', False), outcomePrices = j[0].get('outcomePrices', ['0','0'])
yes_resolution_price = float(outcomePrices[0]) if resolved else None
no_resolution_price = float(outcomePrices[1]) if resolved else None
5. If resolved=true: add entry to dict and write updated resolved_markets.json (atomic write via temp file + rename).
6. Log each new resolution at INFO level.
error_handling: Wrap each market fetch in try/except — log warning and continue to next market_id on HTTP error.
If fetch_open_trades() fails, log error and skip the cycle.
Never crash the process — always sleep and retry.
integration/resolution_watcher.py.atomic_write: Write to runtime/resolved_markets.json.tmp then os.replace() to avoid partial reads by bot_core.
integration/resolution_watcher.py.logging: Use standard logging to stdout (launcher redirects to logs/resolution_watcher.log). Logger name: 'resolution_watcher'.

steps: python -m integration.resolution_watcher  # runs for 10s, confirm no crash, confirm runtime/resolved_markets.json created (may be empty if no open trades)
Confirm only integration/__init__.py and integration/resolution_watcher.py were created — no other files touched

do_not: Touch core/risk.py, core/db.py, launch_all.py, or any existing file
Fetch markets that don't have open trades (no need to poll the whole universe)
Block the process on any single HTTP failure
